program.name: Coles Graduate Program
program.duration_text: Length of program varies by stream (generally 2 years; Finance is 3 years).
program.rotation_text: Rotations are part of the program; rotation possibilities vary by stream (see stream details on Graduate Streams page).

eligibility.work_rights: Australian or New Zealand citizens or permanent residents at the time of application can apply.
eligibility.graduation_window: You'll have completed a bachelor’s degree before starting the program (February 2027 intake).
minimum_requirements: Australian or New Zealand citizens or permanent residents at the time of application can apply.
You'll have completed a bachelor’s degree before starting the program.

stages.name: Online assessment
stages.details: Online assessment includes a video interview; not timed; should take around 45 minutes in total.

stages.name: Discovery Centre
stages.details: Attend a Discovery Centre and take part in a range of activities; every candidate who participates receives feedback whether successful or not.

stages.name: Verbal Offer
stages.details: If successful from the Discovery Centre, you’ll receive a verbal offer; next includes completing pre-hire checks, accepting your contract, online onboarding, and officially starting the program.

exec_themes: Compelling value proposition/value campaigns and expanding everyday value ranges
Operational efficiencies via Simplify and Save to Invest (SSI) and automation programs (e.g., ADC/CFC transformation)
Digital/eCommerce acceleration (growth in online sales, improved digital customer experience)
Customer experience improvement (availability, quality, store look & feel, online satisfaction, price)
Data/loyalty and alternative income streams (Flybuys growth; Coles 360 retail media income growth)
Embedding AI and productivity tools (incl. rollout of ChatGPT Enterprise; AI use cases across operations and customer journey)
commercial_context.profit_engine: Primarily grocery (Supermarkets) sales volume and margin (gross margin/EBIT margin expansion) supported by operating leverage and cost-out (SSI), plus productivity gains from supply-chain automation (ADC/CFC) and fast-growing eCommerce; supplemented by loyalty monetisation and retail media income (Coles 360).
commercial_context.headwinds: Highly competitive operating environment; subdued liquor market with increased competitive intensity; legal/regulatory exposures including (i) significant item provision following a Federal Court judgment related to Fair Work Ombudsman proceedings (judgment received 5 September 2025; additional provision recognized in 1H26) and (ii) ACCC and class action proceedings regarding alleged misleading discounts (245 products, Feb 2022–May 2023); operational disruption risks from extreme weather events affecting supply/communities.
commercial_context.esg: Refreshed FY26–FY30 Sustainability Strategy with three strategic pillars—Climate, Nature, Circularity—supported by foundational commitments including human rights, enabling/developing teams, diversity & inclusion, health/safety/wellbeing, community support, and healthy/safe/affordable food; sustainability targets/commitments include net zero Scope 1 and 2 by 2050, 100% renewable electricity sourcing, Scope 3 supplier engagement targets, deforestation-linked commodity ambition (no later than 31 Dec 2025), and circularity targets (e.g., maintaining >85% waste diversion from landfill; packaging recyclability/recycled content targets).
commercial_context.recent_pivot: Refined/refreshed sustainability approach for FY26–FY30 to align to material issues and evolving regulatory/reporting requirements; accelerated digital and AI enablement (including commencing rollout of ChatGPT Enterprise and broader AI productivity/operations use cases); continued shift toward automation-led productivity as major ADC/CFC transformation investments move from implementation toward benefits realization.

notes: Program dates and intake timing are explicitly stated on the Graduate Program page.
Stream names and stream-level length/rotation details are explicitly stated on the Graduate Streams page.
No explicit program-wide locations or salary figures were found on the sourced pages.
General eligibility (citizenship/PR and bachelor’s completion before start) is explicitly stated on the Graduate Program page.
No additional general requirements (e.g., WAM/GPA, specific graduation year range, disciplines) were explicitly stated on the sourced pages.
The four-step recruitment process and Online Assessment timing are explicitly described on the Graduate Program page.

Profit engine is an interpretation synthesized from disclosed results drivers (sales momentum, margin/operating leverage, SSI benefits, automation benefits) and disclosed growth areas (eCommerce, Flybuys, Coles 360). Coles does not label a single 'profit engine' explicitly in the cited materials.
Headwinds list is limited to items explicitly referenced in the 1H26 results materials (competition intensity, liquor conditions, legal/regulatory matters, weather impacts).

ESG items are taken from the Sustainability landing page and FY25 Sustainability Report excerpted lines; full detailed ESG metrics were not exhaustively extracted due to PDF access timeouts in-tool for deeper sections.
